name: Topical rapamycin human skin aging trial
summary: An exploratory placebo-controlled dermatology trial of topical rapamycin in adults over 40 with age-related photoaging and dermal volume loss.
population: Adults over 40 with evidence of age-related photoaging and dermal volume loss and no major morbidities

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Senomorphics because they can show whether the intervention changes biomarkers, function, and safety in Adults over 40 with evidence of age-related photoaging and dermal volume loss and no major morbidities. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
